name: Nouveau Monde
sentiment: A bold oud-tobacco opening with remarkable depth. The standout masculine from LV's 2016 debut collection.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Noir Extreme, Amouage Interlude Man, Oud Wood
unique: Tobacco and oud over sandalwood — structured and assertive without being shouty.
collector: Worth seeking for the Jacques Cavallier-Belletrud signature.
source: Notes: LV press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: L'Immensite
sentiment: The most approachable entry in the LV fragrance line — a clean, expansive marine-fresh with real longevity for the genre.
similar: Bleu de Chanel, Dior Homme Sport, Davidoff Cool Water
unique: Marine freshness grounded by ambergris rather than synthetic musks — warmer than most aquatics.
beginner: A safe, polished entry point for the LV range.
source: Notes: LV press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Ombre Nomade
sentiment: Widely considered the best in the LV collection. A smoky, leathery oud with dark rose — cinematic sillage.
similar: Amouage Interlude Man, Roja Parfums Enigma Pour Homme, Initio Parfums Privés Oud for Greatness
unique: Saffron-rose top unfolds into a smoky, animalic oud base — one of the great LV signatures.
collector: Benchmark for the house's ambition in the oud space.
source: Notes: LV press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Afternoon Swim
sentiment: A polished sun-dried aquatic — lighter and more solar than L'Immensite. Feels like a private beach, not a gym locker.
similar: Dior Homme Sport, Bleu de Chanel EDT, Jo Malone London Wood Sage & Sea Salt
unique: Heliotrope adds a gentle powdery warmth that lifts it above standard marine freshies.
source: Notes: LV press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Au Hasard
sentiment: The most wearable all-rounder in the LV line — aromatic and woody with just enough iris to feel refined.
similar: Bleu de Chanel EDP, Dior Homme Intense, Hermes Terre d'Hermès EDP
unique: Coriander-cardamom opening gives way to a dry iris-vetiver base that wears close and clean.
beginner: Most accessible of the LV Private Collection for daily wear.
source: Notes: LV press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Bang
sentiment: An underrated pepper-spice woody that punches harder than its price suggests. Divisive bottle, decent juice.
similar: Armani Code, Prada L'Homme Intense, YSL La Nuit de L'Homme
unique: Artemisia-incense midpoint gives a slightly bitter, smoky quality most designer woodies avoid.
source: Notes: Marc Jacobs press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Marc for Men
sentiment: A clean, inoffensive aquatic fougère — reliable warm-weather office fragrance with no surprises.
similar: Davidoff Cool Water, Polo Blue, Dolce & Gabbana Light Blue Pour Homme
unique: Clary sage gives a mildly herbal lift that separates it from pure aquatic clones.
source: Notes: Marc Jacobs press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: DKNY Men
sentiment: A clean, urban aquatic from the late-90s NYC fashion wave — still wears well as a casual office scent.
similar: Davidoff Cool Water, CK Eternity for Men, Issey Miyake L'Eau d'Issey
unique: Artemisia-sage midpoint gives it a slightly wild, herbal edge over pure aquatics.
beginner: Reliable, unchallenging introduction to aquatic fougères.
source: Notes: DKNY press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Fuel for Men
sentiment: Underrated spicy aromatic — the guaiac wood base gives it a smoky warmth that elevates it over most budget woodies.
similar: Spicebomb, Terre d'Hermès EDT, Prada L'Homme
unique: Thyme-nutmeg spice combination is distinctive and sharper than the typical pepper-cardamom route.
source: Notes: DKNY press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Donna Karan for Men
sentiment: Early-90s leather chypre with an aldehydic marine opening — smells of a different era in the best way.
similar: Aramis, Dunhill Desire, Guy Laroche Drakkar Noir
unique: Iris softens the leather into something urbane rather than aggressive — a polished 90s construction.
collector: Worth exploring for the quality of its aldehyde-leather composition.
source: Notes: Donna Karan press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Lagerfeld Classic
sentiment: A well-executed 70s fougère that has aged gracefully. Still worth wearing if you want that era's confidence in a bottle.
similar: Azzaro Pour Homme, Drakkar Noir, Boucheron Pour Homme
unique: Rosewood gives the top a warm, slightly rosy quality before the classic oakmoss-lavender base takes hold.
collector: Representative of 1978 masculine perfumery at a budget price.
source: Notes: Karl Lagerfeld press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Photo
sentiment: A lighter, slightly marine-tinged chypre aromatic from 1990 — sits between the oakmoss era and the aquatic revolution.
similar: Azzaro Pour Homme, Davidoff Cool Water, Lagerfeld Classic
unique: Pepper-marine top over a genuine oakmoss base — a transitional scent capturing two eras simultaneously.
source: Notes: Karl Lagerfeld press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Kapsule Light
sentiment: A pleasant but unchallenging citrus — clean green-tea freshness that disappears quickly.
similar: Acqua di Giò, L'Eau d'Issey, Davidoff Cool Water
unique: Green tea-sage midpoint is the most distinctive element in an otherwise straightforward citrus.
beginner: Inoffensive spring scent at an accessible price.
source: Notes: Karl Lagerfeld press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Boucheron Pour Homme
sentiment: A well-made early-90s fougère with genuine oakmoss and a polished civet drydown. Better than its price suggests.
similar: Lagerfeld Classic, Azzaro Pour Homme, Cacharel Pour l'Homme
unique: The oakmoss-civet base gives it a depth and animalic warmth that modern fougères have largely abandoned.
collector: Worth having for the oakmoss-civet base construction.
source: Notes: Boucheron press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Jaipur Homme
sentiment: A lavender-vanilla oriental that bridges aromatic freshness with genuine oriental warmth. One of the most wearable late-90s orientals.
similar: Kouros, Burberry Brit, Cacharel Pour l'Homme
unique: Iris softens the lavender-vanilla accord into something richer and more complex than standard flankers of the era.
beginner: Excellent approachable oriental for cooler months.
source: Notes: Boucheron press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Initial
sentiment: A clean, dry woody aromatic — a bit anonymous but well-constructed and totally inoffensive.
similar: Armani Acqua di Giò, Hugo Boss Bottled, Paco Rabanne Fahrenheit
unique: Rosewood keeps the top from being purely generic — a warm, slightly floral opening before the cedar base.
source: Notes: Boucheron press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Trussardi Uomo
sentiment: An Italian fougère with oakmoss-chypre undertones — formal and precise. A forgotten classic deserving more attention.
similar: Boucheron Pour Homme, Lagerfeld Classic, Guy Laroche Drakkar Noir
unique: Galbanum in the opening gives it a green, almost aquatic quality before the classic fougère structure develops.
collector: Strong 1983 construction for collectors of pre-IFRA fougères.
source: Notes: Trussardi press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Riflesso
sentiment: A light, pleasant aromatic fresh — competent but plays it safe for the contemporary designer market.
similar: Armani Acqua di Giò, Prada L'Homme, Bulgari Aqua Pour Homme
unique: Cardamom in the heart adds just enough warmth to avoid the wateriness of generic marine freshies.
beginner: Easy spring option from a heritage Italian house.
source: Notes: Trussardi press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Uomo Levriero
sentiment: Trussardi's strongest recent masculine — iris over leather-vetiver is a refined, formal combination.
similar: Dior Homme, Prada L'Homme, Givenchy Gentleman
unique: Leather in the base is subtle enough to add texture without the full leather-forward profile — iris stays dominant.
source: Notes: Trussardi press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Chopard Pour Homme
sentiment: A clean, dry aromatic woody — reliable and well-made without trying to redefine the genre.
similar: Boss Bottled, Azzaro Wanted, Hugo Boss Unlimited
unique: Sage-vetiver combination gives it a slightly medicinal quality that makes it feel more precise than standard woodies.
beginner: Inoffensive, reliable, good for office wear.
source: Notes: Chopard press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Noble Vetiver
sentiment: An above-average vetiver-centric woody — the grapefruit-cardamom opening is more interesting than the standard pepper start.
similar: Guerlain Vetiver, Creed Original Vetiver, Tom Ford Gris Charnel
unique: Grapefruit gives the top a bitter, almost pithy edge that complements vetiver's earthiness unusually well.
source: Notes: Chopard press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: S.T. Dupont Pour Homme
sentiment: A distinguished late-90s aromatic with tobacco and leather lurking in the base — more character than most of its peers.
similar: Davidoff Silver Shadow, Dunhill Desire, Zegna Intenso
unique: Tobacco in the heart — not sweet or gourmand, but dry and almost smoky — separates this from standard lavender fougères.
source: Notes: S.T. Dupont press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Passenger Pour Homme
sentiment: A polished, modern woody spice — saffron adds a muted elegance that elevates it above standard pepper-cardamom constructions.
similar: Armani Code, Dunhill Desire Blue, Hugo Boss Bottled Night
unique: Saffron is subtle — more warmth than spice — making this drier and more refined than the typical mass-market woody.
beginner: Well-rounded modern woody at a fair price.
source: Notes: S.T. Dupont press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Noir
sentiment: S.T. Dupont's most ambitious fragrance — a dark oud-incense oriental with genuine depth at a designer price point.
similar: YSL L'Homme Ultime, Tom Ford Beauty Noir Extreme, Amouage Memoir Man
unique: Oud is used restrained — it adds smokiness rather than dominating, keeping this approachable despite the dark accord.
collector: Good value oriental for the price.
source: Notes: S.T. Dupont press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Cacharel Pour l'Homme
sentiment: An honest 1981 fougère — no frills, real oakmoss, proper structure. Budget access to classic masculine perfumery.
similar: Lagerfeld Classic, Azzaro Pour Homme, Boucheron Pour Homme
unique: Carnation adds a spicy-clove nuance to the standard lavender-geranium accord, giving it more warmth than peers.
source: Notes: Cacharel press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Liberté Homme
sentiment: Light, green, and forgettable — a minor entry in the post-aquatic fresh genre with limited personality.
similar: Hugo Boss EDT, Paco Rabanne XS, Acqua di Giò
unique: Cypress gives the opening a slightly resinous, outdoorsy edge before the generic cedar base takes over.
source: Notes: Cacharel press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Brioni
sentiment: A distinguished, quietly confident woody spice — exactly what you'd expect from a bespoke Roman tailor's fragrance launch.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Grey Vetiver, Hermès Terre d'Hermès EDP, Chanel Égoïste
unique: Papyrus note in the heart gives an unusual dry, almost ink-like quality that sets it apart from standard woody spices.
collector: Well-regarded as Brioni's first and most accomplished fragrance.
source: Notes: Brioni press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Brioni Eau de Parfum
sentiment: Darker and more aromatic than the original EDT — immortelle and tobacco push it toward warm, resinous territory.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Tuscan Leather, Hermès Bel Ami, Loewe 7
unique: Immortelle's curry-like quality blends with dry tobacco into a uniquely Italian-resinous accord.
source: Notes: Brioni press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Basala
sentiment: Japan's defining masculine of the 1990s — a dry, mineral woody with an aldehydic opening unlike anything in the Western canon.
similar: Hermès Rocabar, Chanel Antaeus, Davidoff Silver Shadow
unique: The aldehydic-marine opening evaporates to reveal a bone-dry woody-vetiver base — a structural trick that still feels original.
collector: A pillar of Japanese masculine perfumery. Limited Western availability makes it a find worth pursuing.
source: Notes: Shiseido press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes, Japanese fragrance forums).

name: Zen for Men
sentiment: A restrained, dry Zen-philosophy woody — the Japanese approach to minimalism applied to fragrance is genuinely distinct.
similar: Hermès Eau de Vétiver, Bulgari Pour Homme, Creed Original Vetiver
unique: Ginger-sage midpoint is meditative rather than spicy — the Japanese influence keeps it spare and intentional.
source: Notes: Shiseido press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Shiseido Pour Homme
sentiment: A precise, well-structured 1987 aromatic fougère — the Japanese approach to a classic genre produces something unusually clean and linear.
similar: Fabergé Brut, Lagerfeld Classic, Cacharel Pour l'Homme
unique: Japanese construction discipline shows — the progression is methodical and the drydown is exactly as expected, no surprises.
collector: Interesting case study in how Japanese perfumery approached the Western fougère template.
source: Notes: Shiseido press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: L'Homme
sentiment: A reliable, inoffensive woody citrus — polished enough for the Elie Saab name but unlikely to surprise anyone.
similar: Armani Acqua di Giò, Prada Luna Rossa, Bulgari Aqua Pour Homme
unique: Amber in the heart gives it slightly more warmth than the standard citrus-cedar construction.
source: Notes: Elie Saab press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Le Parfum in Black
sentiment: The most serious fragrance in the Elie Saab collection — oud-rose over sandalwood earns its price at the designer tier.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Noir de Noir, Dior Homme Parfum, Yves Saint Laurent M7
unique: Rose softens the oud into something darker and more romantic — less masculine-aggressive than pure oud constructions.
source: Notes: Elie Saab press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: L'Homme Sport
sentiment: Exactly what it promises — a clean, cardamom-lifted fresh sport scent. Unchallenging and wearable.
similar: Armani Acqua di Giò Essenza, Prada Luna Rossa Carbon, Calvin Klein Eternity Air
unique: White tea in the heart keeps the freshness translucent rather than watery.
beginner: A safe summer or gym-adjacent option.
source: Notes: Elie Saab press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Trouble in Heaven
sentiment: A confident leather-oriental debut — the pepper-papyrus opening opens into a warm leather-vanilla that's far from cliché.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Tuscan Leather, Guerlain Habit Rouge, Serge Lutens Cuir Mauresque
unique: Papyrus in the opening gives an unusual dry, almost smoky ink quality before the leather-vanilla takes over.
collector: Louboutin's debut collection was more accomplished than most fashion house launches.
source: Notes: Christian Louboutin press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Bikini Questa Sera
sentiment: A sun-drenched tuberose-jasmine solar — feels like early evening on the Amalfi coast. Unisex with feminine lean.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Neroli Portofino, Jo Malone London Peony & Blush Suede, Chloé Nomade
unique: Tuberose in a solar context creates a warmth that's expansive rather than indolic — perfect daytime white floral.
source: Notes: Christian Louboutin press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Tornade Blonde
sentiment: A soft, powdery iris-vanilla with a patchouli spine — understated elegance with just enough edge.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty White Suede, Guerlain Mon Guerlain, Maison Margiela Replica Flower Market
unique: Patchouli grounds the iris-vanilla into something more textured and less purely sweet than typical soft woody musks.
source: Notes: Christian Louboutin press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Parade
sentiment: Hedi Slimane's Celine debut — deliberately cool, minimalist, woody-clean. Better suited to the houndstooth crowd than the gym.
similar: Maison Margiela Replica Jazz Club, Hermès Eau des Merveilles Bleue, Bleu de Chanel
unique: Vetiver opens rather than closes — a structural inversion that reads as intentionally intellectual.
collector: Part of a debut collection that redefined what a fashion house fragrance line could be.
source: Notes: Celine press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Nightclubbing
sentiment: The most assertive in the Celine collection — dark leather-tobacco with incense. Aggressively cool and uncompromising.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Tuscan Leather, Serge Lutens Chergui, Comme des Garçons Black
unique: Aldehydes in a leather context create an almost metallic opening — unusual and deliberately challenging.
collector: Strong case for the most ambitious fragrance in the Celine debut collection.
source: Notes: Celine press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Reptile
sentiment: A dry, mineral woody with an unusual aldehyde-patchouli interplay — austere in the best Celine sense.
similar: Chanel Égoïste, Hermès Terre d'Hermès, Comme des Garçons Wonderwood
unique: Labdanum-patchouli base gives a slightly resinous, almost geological dryness unusual at the designer tier.
source: Notes: Celine press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Forest Pool
sentiment: A cool green fir-pine construction — the most accessible of the Celine four, a forest walk rendered with fashion-house precision.
similar: Hermès Equipage, Creed Bois du Portugal, Comme des Garçons 2
unique: Galbanum-pine combination is genuinely green without the soapy flaw that traps most forest frags.
beginner: The most approachable entry point into the Celine fragrance collection.
source: Notes: Celine press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).

name: Pour Lui
sentiment: A genuinely distinctive leather aromatic — the geranium-patchouli combination is unusual, textured, and unmistakably Dries.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Tuscan Leather, Caron Paris Pour un Homme de Caron, Chanel Antaeus
unique: Geranium softens leather into something green-rosy before patchouli takes over — three very different materials that work in sequence.
collector: Consistently cited as one of the best fashion-house masculine launches of the 2010s.
source: Notes: Dries Van Noten press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica, Basenotes).

name: Sens et Bois
sentiment: A refined, creamy sandalwood aromatic — quieter than Pour Lui but with the same precision and identity.
similar: Tom Ford Beauty Santal Blush, Maison Margiela Replica Flower Market, Hermès Eau des Merveilles
unique: Orris-sandalwood combination is deliberately understated — this wears close and rewards proximity rather than projection.
source: Notes: Dries Van Noten press disclosures. Performance: community aggregation (Fragrantica).
